calendar.matters:
Petition To Terminate Trust
Off Calendar Apr-05-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Phil Foster for Vilma D'Altavilla (Trustee/Petitioner).

Mr. Foster stated that the Petitioner filed a withdrawal of petition and that she intends to refile in May of June. The Petition to Terminate Trust is off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition To Terminate Trust
Off Calendar Mar-22-2017 Continued To Apr-05-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Terminate Trust
Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Continued To Mar-22-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Terminate Trust
Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Continued To Jan-25-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Terminate Trust
Off Calendar Nov-16-2016 Continued To Dec-28-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trang La & Matina McDaniel for Vilma Bellone D'Altavilla. Arthur Tan, Guardian ad Litem.

The Court stated that this is a petition to terminate and distribute 2 subtrusts (marital & family) and that there is an indication that the legal and accounting fees are substantial but there is no specifics on cost. Ms. La stated that they have no specific estimate but that there would be over $680,000.00 owed in interest pursuant to P.c. 12003, 12001 & CCP 85.010 and that there would be other penalties. Ms. McDaniel stated that there are some beneficiaries in France, that based on the law there may be negative consequences if the subtrusts were to remain. Ms. La stated that there would be a significant penalty of about 1/3 of the assets. The Court stated that they need to be more specific as to the damages and confirmed that the only assets are the residence and rental property which is 2 units. Ms. La stated that there are issues with the step up basis and capital gains taxes, read the distribution instructions from the trust, that the funding will be valued at the date of distribution instead of the date of death, that $650,000.00 would go into the family trust and the remaining funds to the marital trust and that it is a fractional share of the properties (rental & residence). The Court confirmed the trustee would assign fractional shares and asked what the issues of funding the subtrusts are. Ms. La stated that the beneficiaries would not get the appreciation, that they would have to pay interest and capital gains tax and that the interest paid would be subject to a 40% tax bracket. The Court stated that this is just a general statement and that it requests a declaration that details the disadvantages. Mr. Tan stated that he filed a declaration, that he subsequently spoke to the other minors who are contingent beneficiaries and that they are in agreement with the termination of the trust. The Court stated that it was the intent of the settlors to have the subtrusts continue. Mr. Tran responded that it is possible that when they created this they did not know the complexity and that the grandchildren were not even born at that time. Ms. La stated that the trustee does not want to continue to pay administration and attorney's fees. The petition to terminate trust was continued to December 28, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for counsel to file further detailed support outlining the legal and accounting fees that would occur if the subtrusts continue to be administered and funded. Any documents filed are to be filed in advance of the hearing date.

Judge: Paul Alvarado Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
